chapters.title: Basic Sentence Structure
chapters.desc: This premium grammar chapter helps CPGE students build clear English sentences. It focuses on subject–verb order, complements, negatives, questions and the most common mistakes made by French speakers. The aim

question.q: Choose the correct sentence.
options: People is increasingly concerned about climate change.
People are increasingly concerned about climate change.
People increasingly are concerned by climate change.
question.explain: “People” is plural, so use “are”.

question.q: The report ___ the causes of the crisis.
question.explain: Third-person singular in the Present Simple takes -s.

chapters.title: Present Continuous
chapters.desc: This premium grammar chapter helps CPGE students master the Present Continuous. It covers current actions, temporary situations, ongoing changes, future arrangements, question forms, negatives and the key contr

question.q: Right now, many governments ___ new AI regulations.
question.explain: Use the Present Continuous for an ongoing current process.

chapters.title: Past Simple

chapters.title: Comparatives and Superlatives

question.q: Choose the correct comparative.
options: The UK is more small than the US.
The UK is smaller than the US.
The UK is smallest than the US.
question.explain: One-syllable adjectives normally take -er.

chapters.title: Quantifiers
chapters.desc: This premium grammar chapter helps CPGE students use quantifiers accurately and avoid vague or incorrect claims. It covers countable and uncountable nouns, much/many , few/a few , little/a little , some/any/no

question.q: There is ___ evidence to support that claim.
question.explain: “Evidence” is uncountable, so use “little”.

question.q: ___ government has announced a new measure.
question.explain: A singular countable noun used non-specifically needs an article.

question.explain: Modal verbs are followed by the bare infinitive.

question.q: Choose the most concise version.
options: Due to the fact that prices rose, consumers spent less.
Because prices rose, consumers spent less.
Prices rose, and this is the reason why consumers spent less money than before.
question.explain: “Because” is clearer and more concise than the inflated alternatives.

options: According to me, the reform is unfair.
In my opinion, the reform is unfair.
Following me, the reform is unfair.
question.explain: Use “in my opinion”; “according to” normally introduces another source.
